prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `9b2a60aa`.
codex_response:
Implemented `task_9b2a60aa`. The rule is: find the one multi-cell red template and the singleton markers on one row or one column, sort those markers along that line, then place recolored copies in the same direction as the marker ordering so each blank gap between consecutive 3x3 copies is `marker_gap - 1`. The red marker is the end anchor, so the original red template is the end copy in the sequence.

I treated the summary hints as corrected-but-not-trusted. They got the template/marker idea right, but they were wrong about perpendicular/direct marker-positioned placement, so I documented the correction in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_9b2a60aa/notes.md#L1). No new primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: `4/4` official examples passed, the shared build produced 1000 examples and all artifacts, and `1000/1000` generated examples passed verification. The generated samples I spot-checked from the built set stayed in-family: sparse black grids, red 3x3 motif variants, 4-5 singleton markers, and end-anchored copy chains.
